title: Logical Access — Asset Inventory
description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.
guidance: Evidence: IAM user inventory, role inventory, access key list with last-used dates. All principals should be accounted for and actively used.

title: Logical Access — Credential Registration
description: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity.
guidance: Evidence: No root access keys present. Each user holds at most one active access key. Programmatic access is scoped to purpose.

title: Logical Access — Access Removal
description: The entity removes access to protected information assets when appropriate (e.g., the individual is no longer associated with the entity).
guidance: Evidence: No IAM users inactive beyond threshold. No access keys unused beyond threshold. No roles unassumed beyond threshold. Root account not used for routine operations.

title: Logical Access — External Threat Controls
description: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries, including MFA enforcement, least privilege, and network perimeter controls.
guidance: Evidence: MFA enabled for root and all IAM console users. No wildcard Action in role policies. No wildcard trust principals. S3 public access blocked. No security groups allow unrestricted SSH or RDP. RDS instances are not publicly accessible.

title: Logical Access — Transmission Encryption
description: The entity restricts the transmission of confidential information to authorized internal and external users and protects it during transmission.
guidance: Evidence: S3 buckets enforce HTTPS-only via bucket policy (aws:SecureTransport condition).

title: Logical Access — Encryption at Rest
description: The entity implements controls to prevent unauthorized access to data at rest through encryption of storage resources.
guidance: Evidence: S3 buckets encrypted with KMS. KMS keys have automatic annual rotation enabled. RDS instances and EBS volumes have storage encryption enabled.

title: System Operations — Configuration Change Detection
description: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify changes to configurations that result in the introduction of new vulnerabilities.
guidance: Evidence: CloudTrail enabled with log file validation and KMS encryption in all regions.

title: System Operations — Anomaly Detection
description: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives.
guidance: Evidence: GuardDuty and Security Hub enabled in all regions. VPC Flow Logs enabled on all VPCs.

title: Change Management — Authorized Changes
description: The entity authorizes, approves, and communicates changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its change management objectives.
guidance: Evidence: All merged pull requests have required approvals. No self-merges by PR authors. Branch protection rules enforced on default branches.

title: Ensure no root account access key exists
description: The root account is the most privileged AWS account. Access keys provide programmatic access and, if compromised, cannot be easily revoked. Root access keys should not exist.
guidance: Delete any root access keys. Use IAM roles with least-privilege policies for programmatic access instead.

title: Ensure MFA is enabled for the root account
description: The root account is the most privileged AWS account. Multi-factor authentication adds an extra layer of protection on top of username and password.
guidance: Enable hardware MFA or virtual MFA on the root account. Hardware MFA is preferred for root.

title: Eliminate use of the root user for administrative and daily tasks
description: With the creation of an AWS account, a root user is created that cannot be restricted in any way. Use of this account should be limited to only a small number of tasks that require root user credentials.
guidance: Verify no API calls were made using root credentials in the past 90 days via CloudTrail. Use IAM users or roles for all routine operations.

title: Ensure MFA is enabled for all IAM users with a console password
description: MFA adds an extra layer of protection on top of a username and password. With MFA enabled, when a user signs in to the AWS Management Console, they are prompted for their username and password plus an authentication code from their MFA device.
guidance: Enable MFA for every IAM user that has console access. Consider using IAM Identity Center for SSO instead of IAM users.

title: Ensure credentials unused for 45 days or greater are disabled
description: AWS IAM users can access AWS resources using different types of credentials. It is recommended that all credentials that have been unused in 90 or more days be deactivated or removed.
guidance: Disable or remove IAM users with no console activity in 90+ days. Deactivate access keys unused for 90+ days, then delete after confirming no dependency.

title: Ensure access keys are rotated every 90 days or less
description: Access keys consist of an access key ID and secret access key. Rotating access keys reduces the window of opportunity for keys that are compromised without your knowledge.
guidance: Create a new access key, update the consuming workload, then deactivate and delete the old key.

title: Ensure IAM policies are attached only to groups or roles
description: By default, IAM users, groups, and roles have no access to AWS resources. Assigning policies directly to individual users is harder to manage at scale.
guidance: Remove directly-attached user policies and instead assign permissions via roles or groups.

title: Ensure IAM policies that allow full administrative privileges are not attached
description: IAM policies are the means by which privileges are granted to users, groups, or roles. Policies that grant full administrative privileges provide excessive and unnecessary permissions.
guidance: Replace Action: '*' or Resource: '*' with scoped action lists and specific resource ARNs.

title: Ensure S3 Bucket Policy enforces HTTPS-only requests
description: S3 bucket policies should deny any HTTP request to protect data in transit from interception.
guidance: Add a bucket policy with a Deny statement on aws:SecureTransport: false.

title: Ensure S3 buckets are configured with Block Public Access
description: Amazon S3 Block Public Access provides controls to ensure that S3 resources do not allow public access. Enabling all four Block Public Access settings is recommended.
guidance: Enable BlockPublicAcls, IgnorePublicAcls, BlockPublicPolicy, and RestrictPublicBuckets on every bucket and at the account level.

title: Ensure rotation for customer-created symmetric CMKs is enabled
description: AWS Key Management Service allows customers to rotate the backing key material for a customer-created customer master key. Automatic key rotation should be enabled.
guidance: Enable annual automatic rotation on all customer-managed KMS keys. AWS-managed keys rotate automatically and are not flagged.

title: Ensure no security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to port 22
description: Security groups provide stateful filtering of ingress and egress network traffic to AWS resources. It is recommended that no security group allows unrestricted ingress access to port 22.
guidance: Remove inbound rules that allow 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 on port 22. Restrict SSH to known IP ranges or use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager instead.

title: Ensure no security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to port 3389
description: Security groups provide stateful filtering of ingress and egress network traffic. It is recommended that no security group allows unrestricted ingress access to port 3389 (RDP).
guidance: Remove inbound rules that allow 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 on port 3389. Restrict RDP to known IP ranges or use AWS Systems Manager Fleet Manager instead.

title: Ensure EBS volumes are encrypted
description: EBS volume encryption protects data at rest using AWS KMS. Existing unencrypted volumes should be replaced with encrypted copies.
guidance: Create a snapshot of each unencrypted volume, copy the snapshot with encryption enabled, create a new encrypted volume, and replace the original during a maintenance window.

title: Ensure that encryption is enabled for RDS instances
description: Amazon RDS encrypted DB instances use the industry standard AES-256 encryption algorithm to encrypt data on the server that hosts the DB instance. Encryption at rest should be enabled for all RDS instances.
guidance: RDS encryption can only be set at creation time. To encrypt an existing unencrypted instance: take a snapshot, copy the snapshot with encryption enabled, restore to a new encrypted instance, then cut over traffic.

title: Ensure that public access is not given to RDS instances
description: Ensure and verify that RDS database instances provisioned in your AWS account do restrict unauthorized access by disabling the public accessibility configuration.
guidance: Modify the RDS instance to set 'Publicly Accessible' to No. Also ensure the instance is in a private subnet and the security group does not allow unrestricted inbound access.

title: Ensure RDS automated backups are enabled
description: RDS automated backups support point-in-time recovery and reduce the impact of accidental deletion, corruption, or failed migrations.
guidance: Set a backup retention period greater than zero for every production RDS instance.

title: User Registration and De-registration
description: Implement a formal user registration and de-registration process for granting and revoking access to all systems and services.
guidance: Evidence: IAM users are active and accounted for. Inactive users beyond 90 days are deprovisioned. Access keys are deactivated when no longer needed.

title: Management of Secret Authentication Information
description: The allocation of secret authentication information shall be controlled through a formal management process.
guidance: Evidence: Root and all console IAM users have MFA enabled. Account password policy enforces complexity and minimum length.

title: Review of User Access Rights
description: Asset owners shall review users' access rights at regular intervals to prevent privilege accumulation.
guidance: Evidence: No unassumed roles. Role permissions scoped to actively-used services. No wildcard actions or trust principals. Access keys used within 90 days.

title: Secure Log-on Procedures
description: Where required by the access control policy, access to systems and applications shall be controlled by a secure log-on procedure.
guidance: Evidence: No root access keys exist. MFA is enforced for root and all console users. Only one active access key per user. Root account not used for routine operations.

title: Policy on the Use of Cryptographic Controls
description: A policy on the use of cryptographic controls for protecting information shall be developed and implemented.
guidance: Evidence: KMS customer-managed keys have automatic rotation enabled. RDS storage is encrypted. EBS volumes are encrypted. EBS encryption-by-default is enabled in all regions.

title: Event Logging
description: Event logs recording user activities, exceptions, faults and information security events shall be produced, kept and regularly reviewed.
guidance: Evidence: CloudTrail is enabled with multi-region logging. VPC flow logs are enabled. GuardDuty is active and detecting threats.

title: Protection of Log Information
description: Logging facilities and log information shall be protected against tampering and unauthorised access.
guidance: Evidence: CloudTrail log file integrity validation is enabled. CloudTrail logs are encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key.

title: Management of Technical Vulnerabilities
description: Information about technical vulnerabilities of systems being used shall be obtained in a timely fashion and appropriate measures taken.
guidance: Evidence: Security Hub is enabled for centralised security findings. AWS Config maintains a continuous configuration change history. IAM Access Analyzer surfaces over-permissive cross-account access.

title: Network Controls
description: Networks shall be managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications from threats via the network.
guidance: Evidence: No security groups allow unrestricted SSH or RDP from 0.0.0.0/0. Default security groups have no inbound or outbound rules. VPC flow logs are enabled for network visibility.

title: Electronic Messaging
description: Information involved in electronic messaging shall be appropriately protected in transit.
guidance: Evidence: S3 buckets enforce HTTPS-only via aws:SecureTransport policy condition. Account-level and bucket-level S3 Block Public Access prevents accidental data exposure.
